questions.q: What does the coefficient of determination (R²) measure?
options: The slope of the regression line
The proportion of variance in the target explained by the model
The number of features in the model
questions.explanation: R² = 1 - (SS_res / SS_tot). It represents the proportion of variance in the dependent variable that is predictable from the independent variables. R² = 1 means a perfect fit; R² = 0 means the model explains no variance beyond the mean. It can be negative for severely misfit models.

questions.q: What assumption of OLS regression is violated when error terms are correlated with each other?
questions.explanation: OLS assumes error terms are uncorrelated with each other (no autocorrelation). Autocorrelation is common in time-series data when consecutive residuals are related. It does not bias OLS coefficients but makes standard errors incorrect, invalidating hypothesis tests and confidence intervals.
